display.purpose: Hero headline only. Set tight, weight 600, sized to fill the viewport.

headline.purpose: Section openings. The second-tier voice on a page.

title.purpose: Subheaders and card titles. Ranks above body, below section openings.

body.purpose: Reading copy. Capped at 65 to 75ch line length.

mono.purpose: Inline code, addresses, transaction ids. Geist Mono.

pixel.purpose: Protocol artefacts only: amounts, mint ids, version strings. Geist Pixel Square.

button.purpose: Button labels only. 0.8125rem, weight 500, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.06em — one step below Label so the all-caps slab reads as an object, not a line of text.

motion.name: ease-quart-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions (hover, focus). Exponential ease-out.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Button fill/border changes and other base state transitions.

motion.name: button-cipher-pass
motion.purpose: Visual-only label cipher on button hover and keyboard focus; the accessible label remains unchanged.

components.name: Primary Button (Flat Cipher Slab)
components.description: The principal CTA. A shadowless Ink slab on Paper with sharp corners, a precise border, and a brief left-to-right encrypt/decrypt pass over its visual label on hover/focus. Active feedback is a restrained scale compression.
components.css: .btn-primary { position: relative; isolation: isolate; overflow: hidden; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #18181b; color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 24px; border: 1px solid #000000; border-radius: 0; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.2; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), background-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .btn-primary:hover { background: #27272a; border-color: #27272a; } .btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.985); } .btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #000000; outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Secondary Button (Paper Slab)
components.description: Quieter shadowless slab. Paper fill with a Hair border; hover shifts to Band and strengthens the border. It shares the primary button's brief label cipher pass and active compression.
components.css: .btn-secondary { position: relative; isolation: isolate; overflow: hidden; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #ffffff; color: #18181b; padding: 12px 24px; border: 1px solid #e4e4e7; border-radius: 0; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.2; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), background-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .btn-secondary:hover { background: #fafafa; border-color: #71717a; } .btn-secondary:active { transform: scale(0.985); } .btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #000000; outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Primary Button — On Ink (Paper Slab on Black)
components.description: The closing-CTA variant. A flat Paper slab with a Hair border on Ink, using the same label cipher pass and active compression as the light-surface buttons.
components.css: .btn-primary--on-ink { position: relative; isolation: isolate; overflow: hidden; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #ffffff; color: #18181b; padding: 12px 24px; border: 1px solid #e4e4e7; border-radius: 0; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.2; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), background-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .btn-primary--on-ink:hover { background: #f4f4f5; border-color: #a1a1aa; } .btn-primary--on-ink:active { transform: scale(0.985); } .btn-primary--on-ink:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffffff; outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Top-bar navigation links. Slate at rest, Ink on hover. No underline, no active background.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { color: #3f3f46; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; text-decoration: none; transition: color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #000000; } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #000000; outline-offset: 4px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Hero Display Headline
components.description: The signature hero treatment. GT-Standard at clamp(3.75rem, 9vw, 9rem), weight 600, line-height 0.95, letter-spacing −0.02em. Wraps deliberately.
components.css: .ds-hero-display { color: #000000; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: clamp(3.75rem, 9vw, 9rem); font-weight: 600; line-height: 0.95; letter-spacing: -0.02em; margin: 0; }

components.name: Pixel Notation
components.description: Inline protocol artefact set in Geist Pixel Square. Use for amounts, mint ids, version strings inside human prose.
components.html: Your balance is 21,000 sats across two mints.
components.css: .ds-body { color: #000000; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; max-width: 65ch; } .ds-pixel { font-family: 'GeistPixelSquare', 'Geist Mono', monospace; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: 0.04em; }

components.name: Hairline Divider
components.description: The system's only structural separator. A single 1px line in Hair, full content width.

components.name: Placeholder Surface
components.description: Temporary surface awaiting a real asset (hero image, mint screenshot). Chalk background, Fog caption, 16px radius. Replace before shipping; never final.
components.html: hero image placeholder
components.css: .ds-placeholder { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; aspect-ratio: 16 / 9; width: 100%; background: #f4f4f5; color: #a1a1aa; border-radius: 16px; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; }

components.name: Twilight Stack (Closing CTA)
components.description: The page-closing CTA pattern. Ink section with a monochrome radial bloom anchored just below the lower edge and a fractal-noise paper-grain overlay. Carries the .btn-primary--on-ink slab as its single CTA. Reserved for closing-CTA and footer surfaces — not a card, not a hero, never on Paper.
components.html:
Cashu, in your pocket.

Get a wallet
components.css: .ds-twilight { position: relative; isolation: isolate; overflow: hidden; padding: 80px 32px; background: #000000; color: #ffffff; } .ds-twilight__bloom { position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; background: radial-gradient(100% 60% at 50% 110%, #a1a1aa 0%, #71717a 18%, #3f3f46 34%, #18181b 58%, #000000 80%); } .ds-twilight__grain { position: absolute; inset: 0; pointer-events: none; opacity: 0.04; mix-blend-mode: overlay; background-image: url("data:image/svg+xml;utf8,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' width='160' height='160'><filter id='n'><feTurbulence type='fractalNoise' baseFrequency='0.9' numOctaves='2' stitchTiles='stitch'/></filter><rect width='100%25' height='100%25' filter='url(%23n)'/></svg>"); } .ds-twilight__content { position: relative; z-index: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 40px; } .ds-twilight__title { font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: clamp(2.5rem, 6vw, 5rem); font-weight: 600; line-height: 0.95; letter-spacing: -0.02em; text-align: center; max-width: 18ch; margin: 0; } .ds-twilight__cta { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; background: #ffffff; color: #18181b; padding: 12px 24px; border: 1px solid #e4e4e7; border-radius: 0; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.2; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; text-decoration: none; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), background-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1), border-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1); } .ds-twilight__cta:hover { background: #f4f4f5; border-color: #a1a1aa; } .ds-twilight__cta:active { transform: scale(0.985); }

components.name: Demo Panel (UI/API Switcher)
components.description: Demo panel for the four-parts section: a UI/API flip where the API view is a theme-flipping code pane on 44px graph paper and the UI view is a real app screenshot over the tab's fully desaturated landscape ground (the coded liquid-glass mock is retired). Flipped by a square segmented control. Demo content is real spec data; QR codes never theme-flip.
components.html: { "amount": 10, "unit": "sat" }
components.css: .ds-demo { position: relative; aspect-ratio: 16 / 10; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #e4e4e7; } .ds-demo__view { position: absolute; inset: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 40px; padding-bottom: 64px; opacity: 0; transition: opacity 220ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-demo__view.is-active { opacity: 1; } .ds-demo__view--api { background-color: #0a0a0b; background-image: linear-gradient(to right, rgba(255,255,255,0.07) 1px, transparent 1px), linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255,255,255,0.07) 1px, transparent 1px); background-size: 44px 44px; background-position: center; } .ds-demo__code { font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.75; color: #f4f4f5; background: #18181b; border: 1px solid #27272a; padding: 20px; margin: 0; } .ds-demo__toggle { position: absolute; left: 50%; bottom: 16px; transform: translateX(-50%); display: inline-flex; gap: 3px; padding: 3px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e4e4e7; } .ds-demo__toggle button { border: 0; background: transparent; padding: 7px 16px; cursor: pointer; color: #71717a; font-family: 'GT-Standard', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-demo__toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] { background: #18181b; color: #ffffff; }

narrative.overview:
cashu.space dresses an open protocol in the visual register of a published RFC. Tight grid, fixed-width-quoted accents, monumental display type, and almost nothing decorative. The site reads as primary-source material — not a marketing page for a coin, not a fintech dashboard, not a Web3 launchpad.

The system commits to a few sharp moves: massive editorial display type (GT-Standard) carries the page; neutrals stay pure (ink-on-paper, no tint, no warmth); structural elements are minimal — a thin horizontal rule, sharp-cornered shadowless button slabs, generous whitespace. The Geist Pixel accent exists for one purpose: to mark machine-coded artefacts when the spec calls for a notation different from prose.

Color is intentionally absent. The palette is paper, ink, and a graded family of greys. This is a doctrine, not a placeholder. The bitcoin-curious audience reads sovereignty in restraint; any added accent would dilute the signal.

Colour is absent only from the site's own voice: the Depicted-World Exception sanctions colour inside artifacts the page depicts — real app screenshots, the tap-to-pay video, and third-party marks. The demo panels' supporting landscape grounds desaturate into the site's monochrome register.
keyCharacteristics: Monochrome by doctrine: paper, ink, six grades of grey, no chromatic accent.
Display type does the heaviest lifting: GT-Standard at 6rem–9rem on the hero, set tight (line-height 0.95, letter-spacing −0.02em).
Generous editorial whitespace; sections breathe at 80–128px vertical rhythm on large screens.
Flat, sharp surfaces throughout. Buttons use fill, border, active compression, and a brief label cipher pass rather than shadow or lift.
Cashu's protocol artefacts (amounts, ids, addresses) are set in Geist Pixel Square as a deliberate notation contrast.

rules.name: The No-Colour Rule
rules.body: The system uses no chromatic accent. Greys may shift by lightness but never by hue. If a future surface 'needs' colour, the design is wrong — work the hierarchy with type and space before reaching for a swatch. The one carve-out is the Depicted-World Exception.

rules.name: The Depicted-World Exception
rules.body: Colour is permitted only inside artifacts the page depicts, never in the chrome the site is. Sanctioned, exhaustively: real wallet/mint app screenshots in the four-parts demo panels (including the depicted app's own colour), the tap-to-pay video, and third-party marks at their brand identity (language logos, AI-assistant icons). The demo panels' landscape photo grounds sit outside this exception and fully desaturate. Editorial photography reports rather than depicts, so it also desaturates and its wordmarks force to Paper. Anything else falls under the No-Colour Rule in full.

rules.name: The Two-Black Rule
rules.body: Ink (#000000) and Ink Soft (#18181b) are the only two text colours that may sit on Paper. Slate, Mist, Fog exist for legal disclaimers, nav, and labels only — never for primary reading copy.

rules.name: The One Face Rule
rules.body: GT-Standard carries everything readable. Geist Mono is technical notation; Geist Pixel Square is protocol notation. Three families, three jobs, no overlap.

rules.name: The Big-Or-Quiet Rule
rules.body: Type is either Display (≥3.75rem) or quiet (≤1.125rem). The middle ground is avoided. Hierarchy is achieved by jumping scale, not by stair-stepping.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: box-shadow is forbidden throughout the site, including buttons. Separation comes from spacing, fill contrast, or a 1px border.

rules.name: The Hairline Rule
rules.body: Structural separation between sections is achieved with a single 1px line in Hair (#e4e4e7), full content width, never bolder. No double rules, no decorative rules.

rules.name: The Honest-Network Rule
rules.body: Network visualisations and motion are permitted only when they reflect real protocol structure — Lightning Network bridges between mints, peer-to-peer token transfer between users. Forbidden: decorative flow, speculative connections, mints 'talking' to mints (which Cashu doesn't do), or any animation that exists for aesthetic energy rather than to convey a true property of the protocol.

rules.name: The Two-CTA Rule
rules.body: Primary buttons across the site serve two jobs and two only: get a wallet and read the spec. These are the outcomes PRODUCT.md elevates above mint trust, and the visual hierarchy follows. Every other interactive surface — GitHub, blog, tab switchers, secondary navigation — is .btn-secondary or nav-link. If a third 'primary' CTA appears on a single page, collapse one before shipping.

rules.name: The Twilight Stack
rules.body: The page-closing CTA and the site footer share one continuous treatment so the bottom of every page reads as a single twilight band. Pattern: an Ink section with a monochrome radial bloom (origin 50% 110%, stepping Fog → Mist → Slate → Ink Soft → Ink, all within the existing grey ramp) and an opacity-0.04 SVG fractal-noise paper-grain overlay in mix-blend overlay. Reserved for closing-CTA + footer surfaces only. Never on Paper, never on cards, never on heroes.

rules.name: The Demo Panel
rules.body: The four-parts section demonstrates the protocol twice over: a real product screenshot and the code behind it, flipped by a square segmented control (never a rounded pill) floating bottom-center inside the media frame. The API view sits on theme-flipping 44px Hair-on-Band graph paper; the UI view swaps the grid for the tab's fully desaturated landscape photograph with a real app screenshot over it (the earlier liquid-glass mock card is retired). The screenshot is opaque and keeps its depicted product colour: hairline frame only, no drop shadow, no blur, no rounding. Neither ground is the Twilight Stack. Everything protocol-shaped is real spec data. QR codes never theme-flip: black modules on a literal-white quiet zone in both schemes.

dos: Do carry the No-Colour Rule. Greys may shift by lightness, never by hue.
Do set the hero headline in Display (GT-Standard 600, ≥3.75rem, line-height 0.95). The headline earns the page.
Do keep body copy at 65–75ch max width. Long reads are an editorial feature, not an accident.
Do use Geist Pixel Square only for protocol notation (amounts, ids, version strings). It is signal, not style.
Do name implementations by name. Cashu is a directory; wallets and mints appear as plain wordmarks, no logos required at first pass.
Do treat 1px Hair lines as the only structural divider. Sections separate by space first, hairline second.
donts: Don't introduce chromatic accent. Generic crypto landing pages are the anti-reference — neon gradients on black, gradient text, animated 3D coins. Colour appears only under the Depicted-World Exception: depicted app art, the tap-to-pay video, third-party marks.
Don't add Web3 / DeFi tropes: pastel gradients, blob shapes, playful money-flow illustrations. Cashu is bitcoin-native and deliberately not crypto-coded.
Don't dress up privacy. No locks, no shields, no padlocks, no ALL-CAPS 'YOUR DATA, SECURED™' copy. Privacy is a property of the protocol, stated plainly.
Don't use box-shadow anywhere. No drop shadow, inner shadow, coloured glow, or button exception.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a coloured stripe on cards or callouts.
Don't use background-clip: text with a gradient (gradient text). One solid colour, emphasis by weight or size.
Don't introduce a third typeface. Three families exist (GT-Standard, Geist Mono, Geist Pixel Square) and each has one job.
Don't centre body copy. Long-form reading sets flush left, ragged right.
Don't use stock photography of smiling teams, abstract blockchain visuals, or any hero treatment that could appear unchanged on a fintech site.
Don't ship Chalk placeholder surfaces in final layouts. They are scaffolding only.
